senators.name: John Fetterman
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from Albright College, M.A. from Connecticut College, and M.P.A. from Harvard Kennedy School. Mayor of Braddock, PA for 13 years, applying community development policy in one of Pennsylvania's most distressed towns. Harvard Kennedy School training provides rigorous policy analysis methodology. Known for evidence-based, data-driven approach to economic revitalization and drug policy reform.

senators.name: Dave McCormick
senators.nerdReason: B.S. from U.S. Military Academy at West Point and Ph.D. in economics and international affairs from Princeton University's Woodrow Wilson School. U.S. Army Ranger with combat service in Gulf War. CEO of Bridgewater Associates (the world's largest hedge fund). West Point + Princeton PhD in economics represents exceptional academic and analytical credentials. Combines elite military training with doctoral-level economic research.
education: Ph.D. Economics and International Affairs, Princeton University (Woodrow Wilson School)
B.S., U.S. Military Academy at West Point

PA-01.name: Brian Fitzpatrick
PA-01.notNerdReason: B.S. from Penn State University and J.D. from Penn State Dickinson School of Law. Career as FBI Special Agent (11 years) and CPA. FBI investigative work requires analytical and evidence-based skills. Known as a bipartisan member who cites data on climate and energy policy. Solid credentials but approach not distinctly research-driven.
education: J.D., Penn State Dickinson School of Law

PA-02.name: Brendan Boyle
PA-02.nerdReason: B.A. from Georgetown University and M.P.A. from Harvard Kennedy School. Pennsylvania state legislator. Georgetown + Harvard Kennedy School represents elite analytical education in policy. Ranking Member of the House Budget Committee — overseeing the most complex fiscal policy in Congress. Applies rigorous policy analysis to budget and tax policy.

PA-03.name: Dwight Evans
PA-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from La Salle University. Career as state legislator (36 years in Pennsylvania House). Deep community and economic development expertise built through long legislative tenure. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

PA-04.name: Madeleine Dean
PA-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from La Salle University and J.D. from Widener University School of Law. Career as attorney, college professor, and state legislator. Served as an impeachment manager. Legal training and legislative expertise. Solid but approach not distinctly analytical beyond standard policy work.
education: J.D., Widener University School of Law

PA-05.name: Mary Gay Scanlon
PA-05.nerdReason: B.A. from Amherst College and J.D. from University of Pennsylvania Law School. Career as attorney and education advocate. Amherst College provides rigorous analytical liberal arts education. UPenn Law is an elite institution. Deep expertise in education and criminal justice policy. Vice Chair of House Judiciary Committee.
education: J.D., University of Pennsylvania Law School

PA-06.name: Chrissy Houlahan
PA-06.nerdReason: B.S. in industrial engineering from Stanford University and M.B.A. from University of California, Berkeley (Haas School of Business). U.S. Air Force officer. Industrial engineering from Stanford represents top-tier STEM education directly applied in industry. Haas MBA adds quantitative business analysis. Career as engineer, entrepreneur, and teacher. Brings genuine engineering analytical rigor to defense, STEM education, and innovation policy.
education: M.B.A., UC Berkeley (Haas School of Business)

PA-07.name: Ryan Mackenzie
PA-07.notNerdReason: Career in Pennsylvania state legislature. New member of Congress (2025). Limited public record of analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

PA-08.name: Rob Bresnahan
PA-08.notNerdReason: Career as construction company executive. New member of Congress (2025). Business background. Limited public record of analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

PA-09.name: Dan Meuser
PA-09.nerdReason: B.S. from Cornell University and M.B.A. from Wharton School at University of Pennsylvania. Career as CEO of Pride Mobility Products (mobility equipment). Cornell + Wharton MBA represents elite analytical training. Business leadership requiring quantitative analysis and data-driven decision-making. Applies economic and business expertise to fiscal policy.

PA-10.name: Scott Perry
PA-10.notNerdReason: B.S. in aerospace engineering from Penn State University and M.B.A. from Penn State. Army Brigadier General (retired) and helicopter pilot with combat service. Genuine STEM credentials (aerospace engineering). However, was a key promoter of election fraud claims following the 2020 election — a direct contradiction of evidence-based governance. Legislative behavior undermines the analytical credibility his credentials suggest.
education: B.S. Aerospace Engineering, Penn State University
experience: U.S. Army, Brigadier General (ret.), Helicopter Pilot

PA-11.name: Lloyd Smucker
PA-11.notNerdReason: B.S. from Washington and Lee University. Career as construction company executive and state senator. Business background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

PA-12.name: Summer Lee
PA-12.notNerdReason: B.A. from Penn State University and J.D. from Howard University School of Law. Career in community organizing and state legislature. Deep policy engagement on labor and civil rights but approach is more advocacy-driven than analytically rigorous.
education: J.D., Howard University School of Law

PA-13.name: John Joyce
PA-13.nerdReason: B.S. from Penn State University and M.D. from Penn State College of Medicine. Career as physician and surgeon. Medical degree provides rigorous scientific and analytical foundation. Applies medical expertise to healthcare policy. Serves on House Energy and Commerce Committee.
education: M.D., Penn State College of Medicine

PA-14.name: Guy Reschenthaler
PA-14.notNerdReason: B.A. from Washington and Jefferson College and J.D. from Duquesne University School of Law. Career as U.S. Navy JAG officer and attorney. Legal and military background. Pennsylvania state judge. Solid analytical credentials but approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., Duquesne University School of Law

PA-15.name: Glenn Thompson
PA-15.notNerdReason: B.S. from Penn State University (nutrition) and M.S. from Penn State. Career as recreational therapist and state legislator. Chairman of House Agriculture Committee. Healthcare and rehabilitation background provides some clinical analytical skills. Deep agriculture policy expertise. Solid but approach not distinctly research-driven.

PA-16.name: Mike Kelly
PA-16.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Notre Dame. Career as car dealership owner (Kelly Automotive Group). Business background with Notre Dame foundation. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

PA-17.name: Chris Deluzio
PA-17.nerdReason: B.A. from the United States Naval Academy and J.D. from University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) School of Law. U.S. Navy Reserve officer. Career as election security and democracy attorney. Naval Academy provides rigorous analytical foundation. UCLA Law is an elite institution. Applies legal and national security expertise to election security and workers' rights policy.
experience: Policy Director, Cyber and Infrastructure Security
